Annexes](#page-199-0) ## Page 51 # 3.1 ESG at Gestamp ## Page 51 Sustainability has formed one of the strategic focuses of Gestamp ever since the very beginning, evidenced by its commitment to designing and manufacturing parts that make vehicles safer for people and more efficient, helping reduce CO2 emissions throughout their service life. ## Page 51 Gestamp also actively identifies its primary environmental impacts, as well as the related risks and opportunities. On that basis, it develops policies, management systems and procedures to maximise the positive impacts/reduce the negative impacts of its production activities; as such, it is able to minimise its risk exposure and take advantage of any corresponding opportunities. ## Page 51 Image /page/50/Picture/14 description: The image shows a road running through a forest, with a car driving on it. The left side of the image is overlaid with a picture of the Earth. ## Page 52 Image /page/51/Picture/0 description: The image shows the logo for Gestamp. The word "Gestamp" is written in a bold, sans-serif font in a dark blue color. To the right of the word is a blue square containing a stylized graphic of a curved line with a circle at the end, also in white. ## Page 52 [INDEX](#page-1-0) ## Page 52 ## STAKEHOLDER VALUE CREATION ## Page 52 Gestamp aims to create long-term value for its stakeholder groups by focusing on the way they interact with society and the environment, seeking to create bonds and prioritising the development of direct lines of dialogue and communication to promote a cooperative space in which it is possible to express concerns. ## Page 52 | 1. ## Page 53

## CONTRIBUTION TO SUSTAINABLE DEVELOPMENT GOALS (SDG)

## Page 53

Gestamp utilises its business activities to maximise its contribution to the United Nation's Sustainable Development Goals, with this being possible mainly thanks to its commitment to sustainable industry, the circular economy, employment creation and the fight against climate change.

## Page 53

In order to help achieve these goals the company follows the SDG Compass2 guide, using it to choose which SDGs to prioritise within the company based on their impact along the entire supply chain: design and development, manufacture, use and end of service life.

## Page 53

**[More information on Gestamp's contribution to the Sustainable Development Goals](https://www.gestamp.com/Sostenibilidad/Objetivos-de-Desarrollo-Sostenible)**

The icons are arranged around the words "SUSTAINABLE DEVELOPMENT GOALS" in blue text. Each goal is represented by a different color and a unique icon. Goal 3, "Good Health and Well-Being," is represented by a green icon with a heartbeat symbol. Goal 13, "Climate Action," is represented by a green icon with an eye looking at the earth. Goal 12, "Responsible Consumption and Production," is represented by an orange icon with an infinity symbol. Goal 11, "Sustainable Cities and Communities," is represented by an orange icon with buildings. Goal 9, "Industry, Innovation, and Infrastructure," is represented by an orange icon with gears. Goal 8, "Decent Work and Economic Growth," is represented by a maroon icon with a graph. Goal 17, "Partnerships for the Goals," is represented by a light blue icon with a flower. Goal 16, "Peace, Justice, and Strong Institutions," is represented by a light blue icon with a dove. Goal 14, "Life Below Water," is represented by a light blue icon with a fish. The image also includes the Spanish translations of goals 11, 12, and 13.

## Page 53 sustainable transport systems. ## Page 53 Image /page/52/Picture/27 description: The image shows a text excerpt stating that Gestamp promotes the efficient use of natural resources, works to reduce the waste it generates, and mitigates the adverse effects on the environment caused during the entire lifecycle of its products. ## Page 53 [Chapter on Environmental Management](#page-84-0) [Chapter on Circularity](#page-75-0) ## Page 53 Gestamp fights against climate change by adopting mitigation and adaptation measures. ## Page 53 [Chapter on Road to Neutrality](#page-60-0) ## Page 53 2Developed by the UN Global Compact, the WBCSD and GRI. ## Page 54 Image /page/53/Picture/0 description: The image shows the logo for Gestamp. To the right of the word is a stylized graphic, also in dark blue, that resembles a curved line with a small circle at the end, suggesting movement or connection. ## Page 54 Image /page/53/Picture/3 description: The image shows the word "INDEX" in blue, with three horizontal blue lines above it. The lines are of equal length and are stacked vertically, resembling a hamburger menu icon. ## Page 54 [1. Letter from the Chairman](#page-2-0) ## Page 54 [2. Gestamp Group](#page-4-0) ## Page 54 [3. ESG Perspective](#page-49-0) ## Page 54 [4. Environmental Dimension](#page-59-0) ## Page 54 [5. Social Dimension](#page-107-0) ## Page 54 [6. Governance](#page-155-0) ## Page 54 [7. Annexes](#page-199-0) ## Page 54 # 3.2 ESG strategy ## Page 54 Ever since Gestamp was founded, sustainability has always been one of its key strategic pillar. In the design, development and manufacture of parts for vehicles, Gestamp remains faithful to its commitment to producing vehicles that are safer - with a view to keeping passengers and pedestrians safe - and lighter, while reducing CO2 emissions during the vehicle's service life. ## Page 54 Gestamp views sustainability as a way of creating long-term value in a bid to boost the growth strategy and enable all stakeholder groups to gain a competitive edge. ## Page 54 As the interests of its stakeholder groups figure ever more prominently in its long-term vision, Gestamp subjects all its operations to certain environmental, social and governance-related criteria. This approach gives Gestamp an essential competitive edge and enables the company to take its place among the major players of the sector. ## Page 54 ## DOUBLE MATERIALITY ANALYSIS ## Page 54 In 2023, with the guidance of an external consultant, Gestamp conducted a double materiality analysis based on GRI standards, the Corporate Sustainability Reporting Directive (CSRD) and the reporting standards (ESRS) developed by EFRAG. ## Page 54 The primary aim of this analysis is to identify and assess the main positive and negative environmental and social impacts of the company's activities (impact materiality) and the related risks and opportunities that may arise for the company (financial materiality). It can then set out to define its management model, strategy and action plan accordingly. ## Page 54 To this end, according to a particular methodology (see Annex: Double Materiality Methodology) involving a qualitative assessment of Impacts, Risks and Opportunities (IROs), it was possible to identify and assess, by way of a bottom-up approach, sub-topics, on the one hand, and the most relevant ESG topics for the company, on the other hand. The company's materiality matrix has been produced on the basis of the results: ## Page 54 Image /page/53/Figure/19 description: This image is a materiality matrix, plotting different environmental, social, and governance (ESG) factors based on their impact and financial materiality. The x-axis represents financial materiality, and the y-axis represents impact materiality. The matrix is divided into four quadrants: High Impact Materiality, High Materiality, Low Materiality, and High Financial Materiality. Several ESG factors are plotted on the matrix. 'Water' is in the top left quadrant. 'Responsible supply chain' is in the top center. 'Occupational health and safety', 'Business conduct', 'Own workforce', 'Product innovation and quality', 'Climate Change', and 'Circular economy' are in the top right quadrant. 'Affected communities', 'Pollution', and 'Biodiversity and ecosystems' are in the bottom left quadrant. The image also includes a legend indicating the colors used for environmental, social, and governance factors, as well as a symbol for the scope of materiality. ## Page 55 Image /page/54/Picture/0 description: The image shows the logo for Gestamp. The word "Gestamp" is written in a bold, sans-serif font in blue. To the right of the word is a blue square containing a white graphic that resembles a stylized "G" or a curved line with a circle at the end. ## Page 55 Image /page/54/Picture/3 description: The image shows the word "INDEX" in blue, with three horizontal blue lines above it. The lines are of equal length and are stacked vertically. ## Page 55 [1. Letter from the Chairman](#page-2-0)

## Page 55

This way, **8 topics** considered to be material are identified, either due to their financial materiality or impact materiality:

## Page 55

#### OWN WORKFORCE

## Page 55

Management of direct employees and of workers not directly employed but whose work is controlled by the company (subcontracted).

## Page 55

#### **Sub-topics**

## Page 55

- **•** Attracting and retaining employees and employee loyalty.
- **•** Freedom of association.
- **•** Diversity and inclusion.
- **•** Human rights in the workplace.

#### CLIMATE CHANGE

## Page 55

Measures and strategies to reduce the company's impact on Climate Change (GHG emissions)2 and to develop its capacity to adapt and respond to physical and transitionrelated risks.

## Page 55

#### **Sub-topics**

## Page 55

- **•** Climate Strategy.
- **•** Energy Efficiency.
- **•** Helping to decarbonise transport.

## BUSINESS CONDUCT

## Page 55

Development of policies, control systems and measures introduced by Gestamp to ensure matters such as business ethics, corporate culture or information security are duly addressed.

#### **Sub-topics**

## Page 55

- **•** Good governance.
- **•** Risk and opportunity management.
- **•** Transparency.
- **•** Ethics, Integrity, Corruption and Bribery.
- **•** Regulatory compliance.
- **•** Cybersecurity.

A white dove holding an olive branch and a gavel are in the center of the logo.

## Page 55

#### CIRCULAR ECONOMY

## Page 55

Design of lighter products and development of production processes that promote: the efficient use of resources and the recovery of waste for the purpose of manufacturing components with a high content of recycled material.

## Page 55

#### **Sub-topics**

## Page 55

- **•** Responsible use of raw materials.
- **•** Waste management.
- **•** Product life cycle.

# **ODS**

#### OCCUPATIONAL HEALTH AND SAFETY

## Page 55 Prevention, mitigation and reduction of occupational accidents and illnesses, as well as their frequency and severity, as a way of promoting employee well-being. ## Page 55 #### **Sub-topics** ## Page 55 **•** Occupational Health and Safety. ## Page 55 | ODS | Image: 3 GOOD HEALTH AND WELL-BEING | Image: 8 DECENT WORK AND ECONOMIC GROWTH | |-----|-------------------------------------|------------------------------------------| |-----|-------------------------------------|------------------------------------------| ## Page 55 #### WATER ## Page 55 Responsible and efficient use of water resources in operations, by treating water appropriately and reducing the water footprint. ## Page 55 #### **Sub-topics** ## Page 55 **•** Water management. ## Page 55 | ODS | | |-----|----------------------------------| | | 6 CLEAN WATER AND SANITATION | ## Page 55 #### 2GHG: Greenhouse Gases ## Page 99 Image /page/98/Picture/0 description: The image shows the logo for Gestamp. The word "Gestamp" is written in a bold, dark blue font. To the right of the word is a square containing a stylized white design that resembles a curved line with a circle at the end, all set against a dark blue background. ## Page 99 2023 ## Page 99 #### [4.2 Cicularity](#page-75-0) **[4.3 Environmental Management](#page-84-0)** [4. ENVIRONMENTAL DIMENSION](#page-59-0) [4.1 Road to Neutrality](#page-60-0) ## Page 99 #### ELIGIBLE ACTIVITIES ## Page 99 [INDEX](#page-1-0) ## Page 99 [1. Letter from the Chairman](#page-2-0) ## Page 99 [2. Gestamp Group](#page-4-0) ## Page 99 [3. ESG Perspective](#page-49-0) ## Page 99 [4. Environmental Dimension](#page-59-0) ## Page 99 [5. Social Dimension](#page-107-0) ## Page 99 [6. Governance](#page-155-0) ## Page 99 [7. Annexes](#page-199-0) ## Page 99 Finally, after the eligibility analysis carried out, it has been determined that the activities that fit the description provided by the Taxonomy of the activities carried out at Gestamp are **3.4 Manufacture of batteries, cells and accumulators and 3.18 Manufacture of automotive and mobility components**. ## Page 99 #### **3.4 Manufacture of batteries, cells and accumulators** ## Page 99 This activity includes in its description the manufacture of rechargeable cells, batteries and accumulators for transport, stationary and off-grid energy storage and other industrial applications. Likewise, it includes the manufacturing of the components corresponding to them (active materials for cells, batteries and accumulators, battery cells, housings and electronic components). In this sense, Gestamp fits this last description thanks to the activity of manufacturing battery boxes or battery casings, which is carried out in the Group due to the company's commitment to electrification. ## Page 99 #### **3.18 Manufacturing of automotive and mobility components** ## Page 99 Described by the regulation as the manufacture, repair, maintenance, renewal, reconversion and modernization of mobility components for zero-emission personal mobility devices and approved automotive and mobility components, systems, independent technical units, parts and spare parts, designed and manufactured for use exclusively in vehicles and buses that meet the criteria established in this section and that are essential to ensure and improve the environmental performance of the vehicle. Taking this description into consideration, Gestamp includes in this activity the Door number Rings manufactured by hot stamping, which reduces the weight of the part and the final vehicle (improving the efficiency of the vehicle in terms of energy consumption) and are intended for electric vehicles. ## Page 99 Image /page/98/Picture/17 description: The image shows a close-up of a complex mechanical assembly, possibly part of a machine or vehicle. The components are made of shiny, chrome-plated metal, reflecting light and creating a sense of depth. The assembly includes various levers, rods, springs, and fasteners, all intricately connected. The background is blurred, with hints of blue and white light, suggesting a modern or technological setting. The overall impression is one of precision engineering and mechanical complexity. ## Page 100 Image /page/99/Picture/0 description: The image shows the logo for Gestamp. The word "Gestamp" is written in a bold, sans-serif font in a dark blue color. To the right of the word is a square containing a stylized graphic of a hook or curved line in white, set against a dark blue background. ## Page 100 Image /page/99/Picture/3 description: The image shows three horizontal lines above the word "INDEX". The lines are of equal length and are stacked on top of each other. The word "INDEX" is written in a sans-serif font and is located directly below the lines. ## Page 100 [1. Letter from the Chairman](#page-2-0) ## Page 100 [2. Gestamp Group](#page-4-0) ## Page 100 [3. ESG Perspective](#page-49-0) ## Page 100 [4. Environmental Dimension](#page-59-0)

## Page 100 [5. Social Dimension](#page-107-0) ## Page 100 [6. Governance](#page-155-0) ## Page 100 [7. Annexes](#page-199-0) ## Page 100 #### **Evaluation of compliance with the technical selection criteria** ## Page 100 For this year, a new study of the Group's activities has been carried out under the definition of the Taxonomy and the financial figures of INCN, CAPEX and OPEX for said activities have been calculated. ## Page 100 To this end, the technical selection criteria provided by Delegated Regulation 2021/2139 of July 4, 2021 have been considered, which complements Regulation (EU) 2020/852, for the environmental objectives of mitigation and adaptation and Delegated Regulation 2023/2485 of June 27, 2023. ## Page 100 #### TECHNICAL SELECTION CRITERIA: SUBSTANTIAL CONTRIBUTION TO CLIMATE CHANGE MITIGATION ## Page 100 | Mitigation
taxonomic
activity | Eligibility Analysis | Analysis of the adequacy of Gestamp's activities to the technical criteria of substantial contribution | |-----------------------------------------------------------------------|----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------|-----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------| | 3.4.
Manufacture
of batteries,
cells and
accumulators | [Image: checkmark]
Eligible according to
the definition of the
activity contemplated
in the Climate Royal
Decree. | In 2022, electric vehicles enabled a net reduction of around 80 Mt of GHG emissions. As the electric vehicle fleet continues to grow, emissions reductions will become even more
significant, playing a key role in achieving the goals set out in the Paris Agreement. Therefore, fundamental parts for the correct functioning of this type of vehicle, such as batteries and,
therefore, battery boxes, are essential.

Following this rationale and based on the comparison of the analysis of CO₂eq/Km and total CO₂eq emissions throughout the life cycle of a conventional vehicle versus an electric
vehicle, we consider that battery boxes are essential for the substantial reduction of emissions. in transportation as the main component of electric vehicles. | | 3.18.
Manufacturing
of automotive
and mobility
components | [Image: checkmark]
Eligible according to
the definition of the
activity contemplated
in the Climate Royal
Decree. | The technical description of this activity includes the manufacture, repair, maintenance, renewal, reconversion and modernization of components that are essential to ensure and
improve the environmental performance of vehicles with zero direct CO₂ emissions described in the Royal Decree. In this sense, Gestamp includes the manufacture of Door Rings
through hot stamping, a production process that reduces the final weight of the vehicle.

The Door Ring is an essential part of the vehicle since it constitutes a fundamental part of the bodywork. Reducing the weight of this part through hot stamping makes the final vehicle
lighter compared to parts that do not follow this production system. This is because thanks to this technology, the number of parts used is reduced and its thickness is reduced, allowing
safer and lighter metal components to be manufactured, which means lightening the total weight of the vehicle, reducing energy consumption and, therefore, reducing CO₂ emissions
(improving the environmental performance of the vehicle). As shown in the analysis carried out on the life cycle of the Door Ring, the emissions associated with the raw material
purchasing, manufacturing and vehicle use stages are reduced thanks to this technology.

Therefore, we consider that the lightening of parts is essential for the vehicle to improve its environmental performance as we show throughout the 2023 Annual Report. | ## Page 101 Image /page/100/Picture/0 description: The image shows the logo for Gestamp. The word "Gestamp" is written in a bold, sans-serif font in dark blue. To the right of the word is a square containing a stylized graphic in white, set against a dark blue background. The graphic appears to be a curved line with a small circle at one end, possibly representing a connection or technology symbol. ## Page 101 Image /page/100/Picture/3 description: The image shows the word "INDEX" in blue, with three horizontal blue lines above it. The lines are of equal length and are stacked vertically, with a small space between each line. ## Page 101 [1. Letter from the Chairman](#page-2-0) ## Page 101 [2. Gestamp Group](#page-4-0) ## Page 101 [3. ESG Perspective](#page-49-0) ## Page 101 [4. Environmental Dimension](#page-59-0) ## Page 101 [5. Social Dimension](#page-107-0) ## Page 101 [6. Governance](#page-155-0) ## Page 101 [7. Annexes](#page-199-0) ## Page 101 #### CRITERIA FOR NOT CAUSING SIGNIFICANT HARM - DNSH ## Page 101 For activity **3.18. Manufacturing of automotive and mobility components**, a detailed analysis of the DNSH requirements of each objective has been carried out: ## Page 101 #### **Adaptation to climate change**

## Page 101 An analysis of the Risks and Opportunities of Climate Change has been carried out in accordance with the criteria defined in Appendix A of the Regulation and taking as a starting point those risks listed in point II of the same appendix in which the plant that develops the aligned activity. For more information see chapter Path to neutrality. ## Page 101 #### **Sustainable use and protection of water and marine resources** ## Page 101 An environmental impact assessment has been carried out on the plant that carries out the activity in which the risks of environmental degradation related to the preservation of water quality and the prevention of water stress are determined and addressed. ## Page 101 #### **Transition to a circular economy** ## Page 101 According to Gestamp's circularity strategy, Certified by AENOR, we ensure: ## Page 101 - **•** The reuse and exploitation of secondary raw materials and reused components of manufactured products (see Circularity chapter) - **•** The design for high durability, recyclability, easy disassembly and adaptability of the manufactured products (see Operational Excellence chapter); - **•** Waste management that prioritizes recycling over elimination, in the manufacturing process (see Circularity chapter); ## Page 101 #### **Pollution prevention and control** ## Page 101 An analysis of the specific activity determined as eligible has allowed us to determine that polluting substances listed in Appendix C of the Regulation are not used. ## Page 101 #### **Protection and restoration of biodiversity and ecosystems** ## Page 101 The activity does not affect biodiversity or ecosystems and is not located in biodiversity-sensitive areas or nearby (see Environmental Management chapter). ## Page 101 #### COMPLIANCE WITH MINIMUM SOCIAL SAFEGUARDS ## Page 101 Gestamp is aligned with the OECD Guidelines for Multinational Enterprises and the United Nations Guiding Principles on business and human rights, including the principles and rights established in the eight fundamental conventions referred to in the Declaration of the International Labor Organization regarding fundamental principles and rights at work and the International Bill of Human Rights. ## Page 101 In this regard, the company has a series of internal policies and procedures that ensure that there are no negative social impacts on stakeholders, such as the Human Rights Policy, the Human Rights Due Diligence Procedure, the Health and Safety Policy, the Anti-Corruption and Fraud Policy or the ESG Requirements for suppliers among others. Information in this regard is provided throughout this report: ## Page 101 | Main Aspects | Chapter | |-----------------------------|------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------| | Human Rights | Human Rights

## Page 103

#### KPI CALCULATION METHODOLOGY

## Page 103

Gestamp has avoided double counting of activities during the analysis process by considering only one eligible activity, since the production of the same part could have fit into several activities.

## Page 103

Regarding the calculation process, the accounting data was taken from corporate financial systems, and the report thereof was additionally confirmed with plant teams. These calculations do not include intercompany transactions, so double accounting has not occurred in this sense either.

## Page 103

**Capex**

## Page 103

#### **Sales**

## Page 103

Represents the proportion of net business volume derived from products or services contemplated by the Taxonomy. The turnover KPI offers a static view of the company's contribution to environmental objectives. Calculation of %:

## Page 103

- **•** Numerator: net amount of turnover that is associated with the economic activities carried out by the company that are taxonomically eligible.
- **•** Denominator: Considers the total volume of the net amount of the company's turnover as stated in note 3.23 of the Consolidated Annual Accounts.

## Page 103

Represents investments in fixed assets of an activity that is contemplated in the Taxonomy. Capex offers a dynamic and prospective view of companies' plans to transform their business activities. Calculation of %:

## Page 103

- **•** Numerator: association of the CaPex intended for the selected taxonomic activity
- **•** Denominator: includes the additions of tangible and intangible assets during the year before depreciation, amortization and possible new valuations, including those resulting from revaluations and impairments, corresponding to the year of the company, excluding changes in fair value. Additions to tangible and intangible assets resulting from business combinations will also be included.

## Page 103

Image /page/102/Picture/21 description: The image shows a person holding a pen and pointing at a document with a graph on it. The graph shows a line chart with multiple lines, and the person is pointing at one of the lines with the pen. The document is white, and the graph is in color. The person's hand is visible, and they are wearing a striped shirt.

## Page 103

#### **Opex**

## Page 103

Represents the proportion of operating expenses associated with activities contemplated by the Taxonomy:

## Page 103

- **•** Numerator: Direct costs considered by the Regulation associated with taxonomic activities
- **•** Denominator: non-capitalized direct costs that relate to research and development, building renovation measures, short-term leases, maintenance and repairs, as well as other direct expenses related to the daily maintenance of tangible fixed assets by the company or a third party to whom activities are subcontracted and that are necessary to guarantee the continued and effective operation of said assets.

## Page 103

In the case of the Opex calculation, because the direct costs considered by the Regulation are not relevant compared to the total operating costs of the year, they have not been included as part of the report following the recommendations of the European Commission.

## Page 104

#### CONCLUSIONS

## Page 104

Gestamp recognizes the importance of establishing a business that is increasingly in line with what is established by the "European green taxonomy". In this sense, although the figures are not representative today, the percentage of eligible sales has doubled compared to last year. Likewise, due to the development of regulations and the integration of Gescrap in the 2024 Annual Report, as well as Gestamp's commitment to the development of more sustainable mobility and the circular economy, exponential growth is expected in the coming years of these indicators.

## Page 104

Gestamp will continue working on this analysis to be able to provide eligibility and alignment figures that are more in line with the reality of your business.

As such, we strive to provide them with the training and tools needed for their professional growth, focusing on upskilling and reskilling to develop their talent regardless of age, gender, race or nationality. ## Page 109 The continuous growth and internationalisation of Gestamp has given rise to significant challenges in terms of culture, organisation and human resource management. The constant adaptation of the organisational structure to the growing needs of the Group, as well as workforce resizing, process standardisation, training in new technologies, talent management and the fostering of the corporate culture have all played a key role for Gestamp. ## Page 109 The double materiality assessment that was performed identified several impacts, risks and opportunities linked to the material topic **Company employees**: ## Page 109 #### IMPACT ## Page 109 | Development of the technical skills of our employees through adapted and
continuous training | + | Image: Green triangle | |----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------|---|------------------------| | Safeguarding of rights in relation to freedom of association or alternative measures | + | Image: Green triangle | | Establishment of a robust due diligence process that allows for the prevention,

## Page 110

#### **[2025 ESG Plan](https://www.gestamp.com/Gestamp11/media/GestampFiles/Sustainability/Estrategia%20ESG/ESG-PLAN-2025-GESTAMP.pdf?ext=.pdf)**

## Page 110

With the aim of meeting these targets, the Human Resources Department oversees the management of our organisational structures and people on a company-wide, divisional, regional and production-centre level through the following actions:

## Page 110

**• Planning, analysis and metrics:** enabling decisions to be made regarding personnel requirements and the most suitable profiles at any given time.

## Page 110

- **• Diversity and equal opportunities:** fostering a shared culture within the company that guarantees and promotes equal opportunities in a transparent manner.
- **• Remuneration and benefits:** based on a shared management model for the entire Group that takes into account the pay reality in the geographical areas where it operates, and that recognises the responsibilities assumed, rewarding both individual and group performance and promoting non-discriminatory decision-making in this area.
- **• Labour relations:** developed in accordance with labour legislation that applies to each geographical area and promoting ongoing and constructive dialogue with workers' legal representatives.
- **• Talent management:** identifying and monitoring people talent with a view to promotion and/or mobility within the organisation.
- **• Selection, training and development** of the skills necessary for people to perform well in their jobs and develop new skills in critical areas for business in the medium and long term, as well as leadership skills to fill key positions in the future.
- **• Occupational health and safety** is integrated at all levels of the organisation, from day-to-day tasks to company decisions, in order to ensure safe working conditions and facilities (for more information, see the section Occupational health and safety).

## Page 110

Gestamp has a range of policies and standards in place to ensure the correct management of its staff, including:

## Page 110

> The Global Equality, Diversity and Inclusion Policy
